meta.title: NBA Stats Trivia
meta.subtitle: Player & team stats, 1991–2026
meta.disclaimer: Illustrative stub data for demo purposes — not live league feeds.

questions.prompt: Who led the NBA in scoring during the 1995-96 regular season?
questions.explanation: Jordan averaged 30.4 PPG in his first full season back from retirement.

questions.prompt: Which team finished the 1995-96 regular season with a 72-10 record?
questions.explanation: The Bulls set the pre-2016 wins record before winning the title.

questions.prompt: Who won the 1999-00 NBA scoring title?
questions.explanation: Shaq led the league at 29.7 PPG during his first Lakers title season.

questions.prompt: Which franchise won the 2001 NBA Finals?
questions.explanation: The Lakers beat Philadelphia 4-1 for their second straight championship.

questions.prompt: Kobe Bryant's 81-point game (second-highest in NBA history) came against which team?
questions.explanation: Bryant scored 81 on January 22, 2006 at Staples Center vs. Toronto.

questions.prompt: Who was named 2007-08 NBA Most Valuable Player?
questions.explanation: Bryant won his only regular-season MVP after leading the Lakers to 57 wins.

questions.prompt: Which team won the 2008 NBA championship?
questions.explanation: The Big Three Celtics defeated the Lakers in six games.

questions.prompt: Who led the league in rebounds per game in 2010-11 (stub figure: 15.2 RPG)?
questions.explanation: Love's double-double dominance anchored Minnesota's frontcourt that season.

questions.prompt: Which team broke the single-season wins record with 73 victories in 2015-16?
questions.explanation: The Warriors went 73-9 before losing a seven-game Finals to Cleveland.

questions.prompt: Who was unanimous NBA MVP in 2015-16?
questions.explanation: Curry made a record 402 three-pointers and revolutionized spacing.

questions.prompt: Which team came back from a 3-1 Finals deficit to win the 2016 title?
questions.explanation: LeBron and the Cavs delivered Cleveland's first major sports title since 1964.

questions.prompt: Who averaged a triple-double for the entire 2016-17 season?
questions.explanation: Westbrook's 31.6/10.7/10.4 line broke Oscar Robertson's single-season benchmark.

questions.prompt: Which franchise won the 2019 NBA championship (first title outside the U.S.)?
questions.explanation: Kawhi Leonard's Raptors defeated the Warriors in six games.

questions.prompt: Who won 2020-21 Defensive Player of the Year (stub: third DPOY)?
questions.explanation: Gobert anchored Utah's top-rated defense before later moves to Minnesota.

questions.prompt: Which team won the 2022 NBA Finals?
questions.explanation: Curry won Finals MVP as Golden State beat Boston in six.

questions.prompt: Who broke the NBA single-game scoring record with 70 points in 2023 (stub event)?
questions.explanation: Mitchell's 70-point outburst vs. Chicago highlighted the pace-and-space era.

questions.prompt: Nikola Jokic led the league in total assists for centers in 2023-24 — which team does he play for?
questions.explanation: Jokic's playmaking hub powers Denver's offense around the two-time MVP.

questions.prompt: Which team won the 2024 NBA championship?
questions.explanation: Boston rolled to an 18th banner, defeating Dallas in five Finals games.

questions.prompt: Stub: Who is listed as the 2024-25 scoring leader in this demo dataset (34.1 PPG)?
questions.explanation: SGA's rim pressure and mid-range game headline Oklahoma City's contender run.

questions.prompt: Which team won the NBA title in Michael Jordan's first championship season (1991-92)?
questions.explanation: The Bulls beat Portland 4-2, beginning their first three-peat.
